# Daily Report — 2026-05-28

## Task Completed
Added `close_problem` tool to `problem_tools.py`. The tool PATCHes the problem
record's `state` to `4` (Closed) and accepts an optional `close_notes`,
`fix_notes`, `cause_notes`, and `work_notes`. It resolves both problem numbers
(e.g. PRB0001234) and 32-character sys_ids via the existing
`_resolve_problem_sys_id` helper, and includes a 404 guard. Registered in
`__init__.py`, `tool_utils.py`, and `tool_packages.yaml` (service_desk,
change_coordinator, and full packages).

## Test Results
- Total tests: 1264
- Coverage: 91%
- All passing: yes

## Security
Pre-existing system-package findings (pyjwt, setuptools, urllib3, wheel) — not
introduced by this change.

## Files Changed
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/problem_tools.py` — added `CloseProblemParams` model and `close_problem` function
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py` — exported `close_problem`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py` — imported problem tool symbols; added all problem tools (list/get/create/update/close) to `get_tool_definitions`
- `config/tool_packages.yaml` — added `close_problem` to service_desk, change_coordinator, full packages
- `tests/test_problem_tools.py` — 12 new tests for `close_problem` (success by sys_id, success by number, state=4 in body, optional fields, 404 guard, not-found propagation, request error, missing param)

## Next Up
Add `list_cmdb_ci_outages` tool (cmdb_ci_outage table; filter by CI, start/end date)
